subject: [PATCH v2 1/5] mm/memory-failure: keep the folio, not the poisoned subpage, locked across split

reviews.author: Zi Yan
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch is correct and does not introduce any issues
reviews.raw_body:
LGTM. Thanks.

Reviewed-by: Zi Yan <ziy@nvidia.com>

Best Regards,
Yan, Zi

2026-07-15.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the memory failure handling of huge pages by ensuring that the folio, not just its poisoned subpage, remains locked across split operations. The issue arises when the mapping is unlocked after the split, allowing concurrent final iput() to evict and RCU-free the inode before i_mmap_unlock_read() touches the i_mmap_rwsem. To fix this, the patch moves the unlock of i_mmap_rwsem to right after remap_page(), before the loop that unlocks and frees the after-split folios, and clears @mapping to prevent it from being unlocked again.

The issue arises when the mapping is unlocked after the split, allowing concurrent final iput() to evict and RCU-free the inode before i_mmap_unlock_read() touches the i_mmap_rwsem. To fix this, the patch moves the unlock of i_mmap_rwsem to right after remap_page(), before the loop that unlocks and frees the after-split folios, and clears @mapping to prevent it from being unlocked again.
